narrative:
Karnataka High Court heard **1,054 of 2,708 scheduled matters**, an overall hearing rate of **38.9%**. That is better than **34.1% on 2026-04-21**, even though absolute hearings were slightly lower than yesterday’s **1,096**, because today’s listed load was materially smaller (**2,708 vs 3,215**). The day’s throughput was therefore stronger in conversion terms, but still left roughly **61%** of the board unheard.

Judge-wise, the strongest high-volume performances came from **R. Nataraj** (**54 heard of 72, 75.0%**), **V. Srishananda** (**67 of 97, 69.1%**), **K.S. Hemalekha** (**49 of 77, 63.6%**), **E.S. Indiresh** (**83 of 145, 57.2%**), and **R. Devdas** (**46 of 87, 52.9%**). Among the heaviest boards, **E.S. Indiresh** and **H.T. Narendra Prasad** delivered the largest raw hearing counts at **83** and **49** respectively, while **S. Sachin Shankar Magadum** heard **53** but also had a separate **91-case list with zero hearings**. Large low-conversion boards stood out for **S.R. Krishna Kumar** (**69 of 260, 26.5%**) and **M. Nagaprasanna** (**77 of 251, 30.7%**).

Stage conversion was notably uneven. **Admission (45.9%)**, **Preliminary Hearing (42.8%)**, and **Orders (39.6%)** converted far better than **Final Hearing (22.7%)** and **Further Hearing (20.3%)**, suggesting that clearance was concentrated in earlier or interlocutory stages rather than mature matters. Versus 2026-04-21, **Orders improved from 34.3% to 39.6%**, **Unknown from 33.4% to 47.7%**, while **Final Hearing** remained weak, only edging up from **21.5% to 22.7%**.

By case type, **WP** continued to dominate the docket with **1,375 scheduled cases** and **468 heard**, accounting for nearly **half of all hearings**, but its conversion rate was only **34.0%**. Better-performing sizeable categories included **MFA (49.3%)**, **RSA (44.5%)**, and **CRL.P (41.4%)**. Strong clearance also appeared in smaller buckets such as **CRL.RP (70.7%)** and **CCC (70.0%)**, while **WA (29.1%)**, **COMAP (30.8%)**, and especially **CRP (8.3%)** lagged. Compared with the prior day, the main case-type gains came from **WP (28.8% to 34.0%)**, **CRL.P (32.8% to 41.4%)**, and **MFA (39.8% to 49.3%)**.
